name: San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76)
name_original: Laguna del Cerro Mesado — circular por la Llanura de San Juan
description: Hike the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76), a moderate 8.7-mile route with 62 feet of gain, passing Casas del Destete and Laguna del Cerro
region_intro: Hiking around Alcázar De San Juan offers routes through the expansive plains and wetland ecosystems of Spain's Castile-La Mancha region. The terrain features gentle elevations, including hills like San Antón, which are home to historic windmills. The area is characterized by its significant lagunar complex, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, providing opportunities for wildlife observation. Best hiking trails around Alcázar De San Juan The most popular hiking route is Don Quixote and Sancho Panza – Windmills loop from Alcázar de San Juan, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This route passes by the iconic windmills on San Antón Hill. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de la Veguilla loop from Laguna de la Veguilla, a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This trail explores the wetlands of the Lagunar Complex, known for birdwatching. Local hikers also love the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76), a 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through expansive plains, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Alcázar De San Juan is defined by open plains, wetland lagoons, and historic windmills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate trails. The routes in Alcázar De San Juan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 29 reviews. More than 334 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcázar De San Juan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alcázar De San Juan?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes in the Alcázar De San Juan area. These include a mix of easy walks and moderate trails, offering options for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: The terrain around Alcázar De San Juan is characterized by expansive plains, wetland lagoons, and gentle elevations, including hills like San Antón. You'll find well-defined paths suitable for walking and cycling, offering a blend of natural landscapes and historic features.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Quixote and Sancho Panza – Windmills loop from Alcázar de San Juan&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.2 km trail that takes about 2 hours, passing by the iconic windmills on San Antón Hill, which can be an engaging experience for children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: Many trails in the region are suitable for dogs, especially those on open plains and well-maintained paths. However, when hiking near the Lagunar Complex, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect the diverse birdlife and natural habitats, particularly in sensitive areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: The region is home to the significant Lagunar Complex, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. You can explore wetlands like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Camino de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt;, which are excellent for birdwatching. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31444134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.9 km path that explores these wetlands.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Alcázar De San Juan are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207303765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76)&lt;/a&gt;, a 14 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Alcázar de San Juan&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 12.2 km.

Q: What historical landmarks can I encounter on a hike?
A: Alcázar De San Juan is famous for its connection to Don Quixote. You can hike to the historic 16th-century windmills on San Antón Hill, which are a quintessential symbol of La Mancha. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Quixote and Sancho Panza – Windmills loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you past these iconic structures.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons also provide excellent opportunities for birdwatching in the Lagunar Complex as migratory birds pass through. Summer can be very hot, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching on the trails?
A: Absolutely. The Lagunar Complex, particularly &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt;, is a prime location for ornithological tourism. You can observe diverse species such as herons, geese, kestrels, avocets, and even endangered species like the white-headed duck. The 3.5 km walking route around Laguna de la Veguilla is specifically noted for bird observation.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: The routes in Alcázar De San Juan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 29 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique landscapes, the historical windmills, and the tranquility of the wetland areas.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While most routes are easy to moderate, some offer longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207303765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14 km trail that takes about 3 hours 30 minutes to complete, offering a more extended experience across the plains.

Q: Where can I find viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: San Antón Hill, home to the historic windmills, offers spectacular panoramic views of the expansive La Mancha plains. Additionally, near the Río Záncara, there's a lookout house (Casas del Destete) providing views of the lagoons and riverbanks, rich in wildlife.
tips: Close to the Río Záncara we find this lookout house that lets us see the lagoons and the banks of the same river. A naturalized area where we find a large number of life and birds.

Unfortunately, as you can see, a lagoon that once had water. An area that many years ago had a house to make the quinterías by the farmers of the area. There is almost nothing left... a little of the collapsed houses.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76)?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 14 kilometers (8.7 miles) long and typically takes around 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: The San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76) is rated as moderate. With minimal elevation gain (around 19 meters), the challenge primarily comes from its length. It's suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: Is the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76) a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a circular route, meaning you'll end up back at your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76)?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact route is not widely documented. However, for trails in the La Mancha Húmeda Biosphere Reserve, it's common to find informal roadside parking near trailheads or designated parking areas at visitor centers or points of interest. We recommend checking local maps or satellite views for potential parking spots near the start of the loop.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76)?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs can vary within the La Mancha Húmeda Biosphere Reserve. Generally, dogs are permitted on many trails, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check for specific signage at the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike in the La Mancha Húmeda Biosphere Reserve, where this route is located, is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the weather is generally more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: As the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76) is located within the La Mancha Húmeda Biosphere Reserve, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on public trails. However, always respect any local regulations or signage you encounter, especially concerning protected areas.

faqs.question: What can I see along the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76)?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter points of interest such as Casas del Destete and the serene Laguna del Cerro Mesado. The trail also offers views of the wind farm, providing a unique blend of natural landscapes and renewable energy infrastructure.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Campo eólico de San Juan, Camino Natural de los Humedales de la Mancha, Antiguo Camino de Manzanares, Camino del Cerro Mesado, and Camino de Villarta, offering a varied hiking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on the San Juan Wind Farm Loop (PR-CR 76)?
faqs.answer: Given its location, the terrain is likely to be relatively flat, consisting of dirt paths, gravel tracks, and possibly some paved sections, characteristic of routes near wind farms and agricultural areas. It's generally easy underfoot, but sturdy walking shoes are always recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the way?
faqs.answer: As this appears to be a less formal route, it's unlikely to have dedicated restrooms or official water refill stations directly on the trail. It's always best to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your hike and plan for restroom breaks before or after your adventure.
